subject: Anthropology
title: Language Ideology
description: A quick, practical guide to Language Ideology.

hook.text: Proper English isn't better grammar — it's the grammar of the powerful. Language rules reflect social hierarchies.

buildup.text: Language ideology is the set of beliefs a society holds about language — what's correct, beautiful, or inferior.

discovery.text: These beliefs shape education, law, and identity. Accents get judged, dialects get mocked, and speakers get ranked.

twist.text: No dialect is linguistically inferior. Every variety follows consistent rules. Stigma is social, not scientific.

climax.text: Language ideologies are tools of power. They naturalize inequality by making some ways of speaking seem wrong.

punchline.text: Standard language is politics wearing a grammar mask.

quiz.question: What is a language ideology?
options: Beliefs a society holds about what language is correct or proper
A method for learning new languages
